task.title: Implement post-commit CodeWiki GC capability
task.summary: Add a source-owned GC application/tool path that classifies and purges eligible CodeWiki artifacts only after archive commit proof and restore-ledger emission.

goal.outcome: Agents and adapters can invoke a safe CodeWiki GC dry-run/purge capability instead of manually deleting tracked `.codewiki` artifacts.
acceptance: GC dry-run reports eligible tracked builds/validation/runtime artifacts without deleting them.
Tracked purge requires archive commit SHA/tree proof and refuses to purge without it.
Tracked purge writes a restore ledger with removed paths and `git restore --source=<archive-sha> -- <path>` commands before deletion.
GC does not purge fail/block/current-policy/current-publication validation reports or active unconsumed handoffs.
Ignored runtime/session handoffs can be cleaned under runtime policy with clear reporting.
Pi adapter exposes the GC capability through a typed tool schema.
Main CodeWiki skill names agent ownership of `.codewiki` GC and the archive commit -> GC commit protocol.
Implementation/task-close output rules require post-commit GC review or explicit defer/block evidence after close/publication commit proof.
Validation/publication guidance distinguishes GC ledger proof from validation/content proof and forbids pre-commit tracked purge.
Tool references include the GC capability and restore-ledger safety fields.
Prompt asset tests assert GC enforcement text appears in relevant skill assets.
non_goals: Do not change task-close validation policy except where needed to require GC safety inputs.
Do not push, publish, or remove remote history.
Do not make generated graph state the source of purge truth.
Do not implement GC deletion logic in skill text.
Do not create container/umbrella roadmap tasks.
Do not weaken fresh validation or task-close gates.
verification: Run stale-reference/alignment/changed audits for touched docs and source.
Run npm run test:smoke and npm run typecheck.

exit_criteria: GC runtime cleanup skips active queued/started codewiki_session_handoff files
Consumed completed/cancelled/external/failed handoffs and legacy runtime files remain purgeable
Targeted GC smoke test proves queued/started retention and completed/legacy cleanup
Full checks pass before fresh validation
audit_reports: task/stale-reference/file-structure pass; alignment/changed warnings limited to generated graph/task-view files and active task health before publication

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Implementation validation must inspect the active-handoff guard from source refs, not builder chat.

next_loop.reason: The next gateway must independently validate implementation evidence and cite checked content proof.

requirements.text: A public source-owned GC path must support ignored runtime cleanup without deleting active unconsumed session handoffs.

summary: Superseding TASK-100 implementation build with active-handoff GC guard

closure: TASK-100 remains open until fresh validation passes

evidence: Regression test reproduces validator-reported queued/started active handoff risk and asserts retention.
Test also preserves positive cleanup path for consumed/legacy runtime artifacts.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: src/application/gc.ts adds isPurgeableRuntimeHandoff to parse handoff JSON and skip active/unknown codewiki_session_handoff statuses.
tests/smoke/hot-retention.test.mjs now covers queued, started, completed, and legacy runtime handoff cases.
notes: Fresh validation fail report was consumed and fixed.
No actual dogfood GC purge performed.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Regression test reproduces validator-reported queued/started active handoff risk and asserts retention.
Test also preserves positive cleanup path for consumed/legacy runtime artifacts.
code_change_evidence: src/application/gc.ts adds isPurgeableRuntimeHandoff to parse handoff JSON and skip active/unknown codewiki_session_handoff statuses.
tests/smoke/hot-retention.test.mjs now covers queued, started, completed, and legacy runtime handoff cases.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: GC does not purge active unconsumed handoffs
acceptance_mapping.evidence: src/application/gc.ts skips codewiki_session_handoff files unless status is completed, cancelled, external, or failed; tests assert queued and started handoffs are absent from dry-run and survive runtime purge.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Ignored runtime/session handoffs can still be cleaned under runtime policy
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/hot-retention.test.mjs asserts legacy runtime file and completed handoff are dry-run candidates and deleted by runtime-only purge without archive proof.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Validation failure addressed without weakening archive proof gates
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Existing tracked purge archive proof tests continue passing; full typecheck/smoke/features/pack checks pass.

closure_brief.user_intent: Fix TASK-100 validation failure so CodeWiki runtime GC preserves active unconsumed handoffs while still cleaning consumed runtime trash.
implemented_changes: Runtime GC now excludes active or unknown-status codewiki_session_handoff files from candidates.
Runtime GC still purges completed/cancelled/external/failed handoffs and legacy disposable runtime files.
Hot-retention smoke test now proves queued/started handoffs survive dry-run and purge while completed/legacy runtime files are deleted.
non_goals_preserved: No tracked GC purge before archive commit proof.
No task close from builder context.

risks: Fresh validation still pending after fix; task-close still requires post-validation commit/immutable proof.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: chore(codewiki): record TASK-100 Implement post-commit CodeWiki GC capability implementation evidence

Superseding TASK-100 implementation build with active-handoff GC guard

Checks: npm run typecheck, node tests/smoke/hot-retention.test.mjs, npm run test:smoke, npm run check:architecture, npm run test:features, npm run test:pack, codewiki_audit profiles=alignment,changed,task,stale-reference,file-structure task_id=TASK-100

Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
